days.title: Week 11 Day 1: Opening a Bank Account

words.example_sentence_target: Je voudrais ouvrir un compte bancaire.
words.example_sentence_native: I would like to open a bank account.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Zhuh voo-dreh oo-vreer uh kohnt bahn-kehr.

words.example_sentence_native: Do you have an ID document?

words.example_sentence_target: Il me faut un justificatif de domicile.
words.example_sentence_native: I need a proof of address.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Eel muh foh uh zhoo-stee-fee-kah-teef duh doh-mee-seel.

words.example_sentence_target: J'ai besoin de votre signature ici.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Zheh buh-zwan duh voh-truh seen-yah-teer ee-see.

words.example_sentence_target: Je vais voir mon conseiller bancaire.
words.example_sentence_native: I am going to see my bank advisor.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Zhuh veh vwahr mohn kohn-seh-yeh bahn-kehr.

sentences.target: Est-ce qu'il y a un dépôt minimum ?
sentences.phonetic: Es-keel ee ah uh deh-poh mee-nee-moom?

sentences.target: Je n'ai pas encore de fiche de paie.
sentences.native: I don't have a pay slip yet.
sentences.phonetic: Zhuh neh pah zahm-kor duh feesh duh peh.

sentences.native: How long does the opening take?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Bonjour, je voudrais ouvrir un compte courant.
lines.native: Hello, I would like to open a checking account.
lines.phonetic: Bohn-zhoor, zhuh voo-dreh oo-vreer uh kohnt koo-rahn.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Bien sûr. Avez-vous pris rendez-vous ?
lines.native: Of course. Have you made an appointment?
lines.phonetic: Byan seer. Ah-veh voo pree rahn-deh-voo?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: J'ai mon passeport et une facture d'électricité.
lines.native: I have my passport and an electricity bill.
lines.phonetic: Zheh mohn pahs-por eh oon fahk-teer deh-lek-tree-see-teh.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: C'est parfait pour le justificatif de domicile.
lines.native: That's perfect for the proof of address.
lines.phonetic: Seh pahr-feh poor luh zhoo-stee-fee-kah-teef duh doh-mee-seel.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Est-ce que je recevrai une carte bancaire ?
lines.native: Will I receive a bank card?
lines.phonetic: Es-kuh zhuh ruh-sev-reh oon kahrt bahn-kehr?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Oui, par courrier dans une semaine.
lines.native: Yes, by mail in a week.
lines.phonetic: Wee, pahr koo-ryeh dahn zoon suh-mehn.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: C'est mon premier compte en France.
lines.native: It is my first account in France.
lines.phonetic: Seh mohn pruh-myeh kohnt ahn frahns.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Bienvenue. Je vais vous expliquer les options.
lines.native: Welcome. I am going to explain the options to you.
lines.phonetic: Byan-vuh-nee. Zhuh veh voo zeks-plee-keh leh zohp-syohn.

multipleChoice.question: What is 'Justificatif de domicile'?
multipleChoice.explanation: 'Justificatif de domicile' refers to any document (like a bill) proving where you live.

sentenceScramble.nativeHint: I would like to open an account

days.cultural_note: In France, opening a bank account is essential for renting a home and receiving a salary. You will often need to provide a 'justificatif de domicile' which is less than 3 months old.
days.pronunciation_tip: The 'oi' in 'ouvrir' is pronounced like the 'oo' in 'boots'. The 'un' is a nasal sound like 'uh'.

days.title: Week 11 Day 2: Deposits and Withdrawals

words.example_sentence_native: I want to make a withdrawal.

words.example_sentence_target: Où est le distributeur le plus proche ?
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Oo eh luh dees-tree-bee-tehr luh plee prosh?

words.example_sentence_target: Ma carte bleue ne marche pas.
words.example_sentence_native: My bank card is not working.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Mah kahrt bluh nuh mahrsh pah.

words.example_sentence_target: Je dois faire un dépôt d'argent.
words.example_sentence_native: I need to make a deposit of money.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Zhuh dwah fehr uh deh-poh dahr-zhahn.

sentences.target: Le distributeur a avalé ma carte.
sentences.phonetic: Luh dees-tree-bee-tehr ah ah-vah-leh mah kahrt.

sentences.target: Y a-t-il des frais de retrait ?
sentences.phonetic: Ee ah-teel deh freh duh ruh-treh?

dialogues.context: At an ATM with a friend.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Je dois retirer cinquante euros.
lines.native: I need to withdraw fifty euros.
lines.phonetic: Zhuh dwah ruh-tee-reh san-kahnt uh-roh.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Il y a un distributeur juste là.
lines.native: There is an ATM right there.
lines.phonetic: Eel ee ah uh dees-tree-bee-tehr zhoost lah.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Puis-je déposer ce chèque ?
lines.native: Can I deposit this check?
lines.phonetic: Pwee-zhuh deh-poh-zeh suh shek?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Oui, remplissez ce bordereau de dépôt.
lines.native: Yes, fill out this deposit slip.
lines.phonetic: Wee, rahm-plee-seh suh bor-duh-roh duh deh-poh.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Ma carte est bloquée dans la machine.
lines.native: My card is stuck in the machine.
lines.phonetic: Mah kahrt eh bloh-keh dahn lah mah-sheen.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Vous devez entrer et prévenir la banque.
lines.native: You must go inside and notify the bank.
lines.phonetic: Voo duh-veh ahn-treh eh preh-vuh-neer lah bahnk.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Avez-vous de la monnaie ?
lines.native: Do you have change?
lines.phonetic: Ah-veh voo duh lah mohn-neh?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Désolé, je n'ai que des billets de vingt.
lines.native: Sorry, I only have twenty-euro notes.
lines.phonetic: Deh-zoh-leh, zhuh neh kuh deh bee-yeh duh van.

fillBlanks.sentence: Où est le ___ le plus proche ?

multipleChoice.question: What should you never share with anyone?
multipleChoice.explanation: Your 'code secret' (PIN) is private and essential for your card's security.

sentenceScramble.nativeHint: I want to make a withdrawal

days.cultural_note: In France, bank cards are commonly called 'Carte Bleue' or 'CB'. Contactless payment (sans contact) is very common for amounts under 50 euros.
days.pronunciation_tip: The 'u' in 'distributeur' is a tight sound made with rounded lips. Practice by saying 'eee' and rounding your lips.

days.title: Week 11 Day 3: Banking Fees and Statements

words.example_sentence_target: Quels sont les frais de gestion ?
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Kel sohn leh freh duh zhes-tyohn?

words.example_sentence_target: J'ai reçu mon relevé de compte.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Zheh ruh-see mohn ruh-luh-veh duh kohnt.

words.example_sentence_native: I would like to make a transfer.
It is an automatic direct debit.

words.example_sentence_target: Quel est le solde de mon compte ?
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Kel eh luh sohld duh mohn kohnt?

sentences.target: Il y a une erreur sur mon relevé.
sentences.native: There is an error on my statement.
sentences.phonetic: Eel ee ah oon ehr-uhr seer mohn ruh-luh-veh.

sentences.native: I would like to change my limit.

sentences.target: Mon salaire n'est pas encore arrivé.
sentences.phonetic: Mohn sah-lehr neh pah zahm-kor ah-ree-veh.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Pourquoi ai-je ces frais bancaires ?
lines.native: Why do I have these bank fees?
lines.phonetic: Poor-kwah eh-zhuh seh freh bahn-kehr?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Ce sont les frais de découvert.
lines.native: These are overdraft fees.
lines.phonetic: Suh sohn leh freh duh deh-koo-vehr.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Je veux faire un virement vers l'étranger.
lines.native: I want to make a transfer abroad.
lines.phonetic: Zhuh vuh fehr uh veer-mahn vehr leh-trahn-zheh.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: D'accord, il me faut le code IBAN.
lines.native: Okay, I need the IBAN code.
lines.phonetic: Dah-kor, eel muh foh luh kohd ee-bahn.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Puis-je avoir le solde de mon compte ?
lines.native: Can I have my account balance?
lines.phonetic: Pwee-zhuh ah-vwahr luh sohld duh mohn kohnt?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Oui, voici votre situation actuelle.
lines.native: Yes, here is your current status.
lines.phonetic: Wee, vwah-see voh-truh see-too-ah-syohn ahk-too-el.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Mon loyer est payé par prélèvement.
lines.native: My rent is paid by direct debit.
lines.phonetic: Mohn lwah-yeh eh peh-yeh pahr preh-lev-mahn.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: C'est plus pratique pour ne pas oublier.
lines.native: It's more practical so as not to forget.
lines.phonetic: Seh plee prah-teek poor nuh pah zoo-blee-yeh.

fillBlanks.sentence: Quel est le ___ de mon compte ?

multipleChoice.question: What does 'à découvert' mean?
multipleChoice.explanation: 'À découvert' means you have spent more money than you have in your account.

days.cultural_note: In France, being 'à découvert' (overdrawn) usually incurs 'agios' (overdraft fees). It's important to monitor your 'relevé de compte' (bank statement) regularly.
days.pronunciation_tip: The 'en' in 'virement' and 'prélèvement' is a nasal sound. Think of the 'on' in 'song' but without the 'g'.

days.title: Week 11 Day 4: Mobile and Online Banking

words.example_sentence_target: J'ai oublié mon mot de passe.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Zheh oo-blee-yeh mohn moh duh pahs.

words.example_sentence_target: Je n'arrive pas à me connecter.
words.example_sentence_native: I can't manage to log in.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Zhuh nah-reev pah ah muh koh-nek-teh.

sentences.native: Download the app on your mobile.
How do I activate my client space?

sentences.target: Je peux tout faire en ligne.
sentences.phonetic: Zhuh puh too fehr ahn leen-yuh.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Où est mon identifiant pour la banque ?
lines.native: Where is my bank username?
lines.phonetic: Oo eh mohn ee-dahn-tee-fyan poor lah bahnk?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Il est écrit sur votre contrat.
lines.native: It is written on your contract.
lines.phonetic: Eel eh teh-kree seer voh-truh kohn-trah.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: J'ai oublié mon mot de passe.
lines.native: I forgot my password.
lines.phonetic: Zheh oo-blee-yeh mohn moh duh pahs.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Cliquez sur 'mot de passe oublié'.
lines.native: Click on 'forgotten password'.
lines.phonetic: Klee-keh seer moh duh pahs oo-blee-yeh.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Est-ce que c'est risqué de payer en ligne ?
lines.native: Is it risky to pay online?
lines.phonetic: Es-kuh seh rees-keh duh peh-yeh ahn leen-yuh?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Non, si le site est sécurisé.
lines.native: No, if the site is secure.
lines.phonetic: Nohn, see luh seet eh seh-koo-ree-zeh.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Je fais le virement depuis mon téléphone.
lines.native: I am making the transfer from my phone.
lines.phonetic: Zhuh feh luh veer-mahn duh-pwee mohn teh-leh-fohn.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: C'est rapide et efficace.
lines.native: It's fast and efficient.
lines.phonetic: Seh rah-peed eh eh-fee-kahs.

multipleChoice.question: What is 'Se connecter'?
multipleChoice.explanation: 'Se connecter' means to connect or log in to a service.

days.cultural_note: France has many 'neo-banks' (digital-only banks) which are popular for their low fees and easy-to-use mobile apps.
days.pronunciation_tip: The 'h' in 'historique' is silent, as in almost all French words. It sounds like 'ees-toh-reek'.

days.title: Week 11 Day 5: Paying Bills and Budgeting
days.title_native: Payer les factures et le budget

words.example_sentence_native: I have a bill to pay.
I want to save money to travel.

sentences.target: Je paye mon loyer tous les mois.
sentences.native: I pay my rent every month.
sentences.phonetic: Zhuh peh mohn lwah-yeh too leh mwah.

sentences.target: Il faut réduire les dépenses inutiles.
sentences.phonetic: Eel foh reh-dyeer leh deh-pahs ee-nee-teel.

sentences.target: Avez-vous mis de l'argent de côté ?
sentences.phonetic: Ah-veh voo mee duh lahr-zhahn duh koh-teh?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Tu as payé la facture de gaz ?
lines.native: Did you pay the gas bill?
lines.phonetic: Too ah peh-yeh lah fahk-teer duh gahz?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Oui, je l'ai fait hier soir.
lines.native: Yes, I did it last night.
lines.phonetic: Wee, zhuh leh feh ee-ehr swahr.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: On dépasse notre budget courses.
lines.native: We are exceeding our grocery budget.
lines.phonetic: Ohn deh-pahs noh-truh bee-dzeh koors.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: On doit acheter moins de viande.
lines.native: We need to buy less meat.
lines.phonetic: Ohn dwah ah-shuh-teh mwan duh vyahnd.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: J'essaye d'économiser cent euros par mois.
lines.native: I am trying to save one hundred euros per month.
lines.phonetic: Zheh-seh duh eh-koh-noh-mee-zeh sahnt uh-roh pahr mwah.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: C'est une bonne habitude.
lines.native: It's a good habit.
lines.phonetic: Seh toon bohn ah-bee-teed.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: L'échéance de la facture est passée.
lines.native: The bill deadline has passed.
lines.phonetic: Leh-sheh-ahs duh lah fahk-teer eh pah-seh.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Vite, il faut payer pour éviter les pénalités.
lines.native: Quick, we must pay to avoid penalties.
lines.phonetic: Veet, eel foh peh-yeh poor eh-vee-teh leh peh-nah-lee-teh.

multipleChoice.question: What does 'économiser' mean?
multipleChoice.explanation: 'Économiser' means to save or economize your money.

days.cultural_note: Rent and utility bills are often paid via 'RIB' (Relevé d'Identité Bancaire), which is a document provided by your bank containing your account details.
days.pronunciation_tip: The 'et' in 'budget' is pronounced like 'eh' in 'pet'.

days.title: Week 11 Day 6: Credit and Loans

words.example_sentence_native: I would like to ask for a loan.
I have to pay back my credit.
The bank is asking for a guarantee.

sentences.target: Il me faut un prêt pour ma voiture.
sentences.native: I need a loan for my car.
sentences.phonetic: Eel muh foh uh preh poor mah vwah-teer.

sentences.target: Le crédit est sur dix ans.
sentences.native: The credit is over ten years.
sentences.phonetic: Luh kreh-dee eh seer dee zahn.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Bonjour, je voudrais faire un prêt.
lines.native: Hello, I would like to take out a loan.
lines.phonetic: Bohn-zhoor, zhuh voo-dreh fehr uh preh.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Pour quel projet ?
lines.native: For what project?
lines.phonetic: Poor kel proh-zheh?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Le taux d'intérêt est-il élevé ?
lines.native: Is the interest rate high?
lines.phonetic: Luh toh dan-teh-reh eh-teel eh-luh-veh?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Non, c'est un taux promotionnel.
lines.native: No, it's a promotional rate.
lines.phonetic: Nohn, seh tuh toh proh-moh-syohn-el.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Combien vais-je payer par mois ?
lines.native: How much will I pay per month?
lines.phonetic: Kohm-byan veh-zhuh peh-yeh pahr mwah?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Votre mensualité sera de trois cents euros.
lines.native: Your monthly payment will be three hundred euros.
lines.phonetic: Voh-truh mahn-soo-ah-lee-teh suh-rah duh trwah sahnt uh-roh.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Puis-je rembourser par anticipation ?
lines.native: Can I pay back early?
lines.phonetic: Pwee-zhuh rahm-boor-seh pahr ahn-tee-see-pah-syohn?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Oui, mais il y a des conditions.
lines.native: Yes, but there are conditions.
lines.phonetic: Wee, meh eel ee ah deh kohn-dee-syohn.

multipleChoice.question: What is 'un emprunt immobilier'?
multipleChoice.explanation: An 'emprunt immobilier' is a loan specifically for buying property (real estate).

sentenceScramble.nativeHint: I would like to ask for a loan

days.cultural_note: French banks are generally quite strict about lending. You will usually need a CDI (permanent contract) to get a significant loan or mortgage.
days.pronunciation_tip: The 'un' in 'emprunt' is a nasal sound. It's similar to the sound in 'twenty' without the final consonants.

days.title: Week 11 Day 7: Card Security and Loss
days.title_native: Sécurité de la carte et perte

sentences.native: I want to report a theft.

dialogues.context: Calling the bank to block a card.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Allô, je voudrais faire opposition.
lines.native: Hello, I would like to block my card.
lines.phonetic: Ah-loh, zhuh voo-dreh fehr oh-poh-zee-syohn.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Quel est votre numéro de compte ?
lines.native: What is your account number?
lines.phonetic: Kel eh voh-truh nee-meh-roh duh kohnt?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: J'ai perdu mon portefeuille ici.
lines.native: I lost my wallet here.
lines.phonetic: Zheh pehr-dee mohn por-teh-fuh-yuh ee-see.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: On ne l'a pas trouvé, désolé.
lines.native: We haven't found it, sorry.
lines.phonetic: Ohn nuh lah pah troo-veh, deh-zoh-leh.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Il y a un achat que je n'ai pas fait.
lines.native: There is a purchase I didn't make.
lines.phonetic: Eel ee ah uh nah-shah kuh zhuh neh pah feh.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Nous allons bloquer votre carte immédiatement.
lines.native: We are going to block your card immediately.
lines.phonetic: Noo zah-lohn bloh-keh voh-truh kahrt ee-meh-dyat-mahn.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Quand recevrai-je ma nouvelle carte ?
lines.native: When will I receive my new card?
lines.phonetic: Kahn ruh-suv-reh-zhuh mah noo-vel kahrt?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Elle arrivera sous cinq jours ouvrés.
lines.native: It will arrive within five working days.
lines.phonetic: El ah-ree-vrah soo san koor zoo-vreh.

multipleChoice.question: What is 'Faire opposition'?
multipleChoice.explanation: 'Faire opposition' is the specific term for calling the bank to cancel or block a lost or stolen card.

days.cultural_note: Every bank has a dedicated 24/7 service for 'opposition'. Make sure to save your bank's emergency number in your phone.
days.pronunciation_tip: The 'u' in 'perdu' is a sharp sound. The 'é' in 'volé' and 'bloqué' sounds like 'ay' in 'play'.
